name: market_open_boundary
description: time_ratio = 0.0 (market open). Under the M3 re-derivation f(t) = 1 - sqrt(1 - t), decay_curve = 1 - sqrt(1) = 0 EXACTLY, so dynamic_multiplier collapses to MULT_OPEN and dynamic_min_stop collapses to MIN_STOP_OPEN. Pins the lower-boundary regime: at open, the time-squeeze layer applies the widest stop (1.5x vol multiplier, 0.30% minimum floor). A wrong impl that left the old log10 formula in place would still pass at this boundary (log10(1) is also 0), so this fixture is paired with fixture 03 (t=0.5) where the two formulas diverge by ~0.45 in decay.
provenance: DERIVED from the closed-form THEORY formula f(t) = 1 - sqrt(1 - t) (Danielsson & Zigrand, 2003, LSE FMG DP-439, the i.i.d.-returns square-root-of-time remaining-variance curve). NOT captured from compute_time_squeeze_decay output -- D-2 load-bearing per docs/research/m3-provenance/literature-pass.md §3.1.
derivation: decay_curve = 1 - sqrt(1 - 0.0) = 1 - sqrt(1) = 1 - 1 = 0.0 EXACTLY. dynamic_multiplier = MULT_OPEN - (MULT_OPEN - MULT_CLOSE) * decay_curve = 1.5 - (1.5 - 0.5) * 0.0 = 1.5 - 1.0 * 0.0 = 1.5. dynamic_min_stop = MIN_STOP_OPEN - (MIN_STOP_OPEN - MIN_STOP_CLOSE) * decay_curve = 0.3 - (0.3 - 0.15) * 0.0 = 0.3 - 0.15 * 0.0 = 0.3.
